results.prompt: Set up an AI agent that monitors device health and creates ServiceNow tickets for issues. I have a Claude provider already configured.

assertions.text: Agent has identity with agent_account and agent_password
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 254-256 show identity with agent_account and agent_password in the create example. Line 291 documents these fields explicitly.

assertions.text: llm.provider references an existing provider name
assertions.evidence: Skill line 258 shows 'provider': 'Production Claude' in the example. Line 293 documents 'llm.provider -- name of a provider instance'.

assertions.text: messages array has system prompt first, then user objective
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 263-270 show messages with system role first, then user role. Line 595 states 'messages array order matters: system prompt first, then user objective'.
Skill line 585 states 'Tool identifiers use // as separator: adapter_name//method_name'. Lines 277-278 show examples like 'ServiceNow//createChangeRequest'.
Skill lines 248-285 show the full create agent example wrapped in {"details": {...}}.

assertions.text: capabilities.toolset is an array of tool identifiers
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 276-279 show capabilities.toolset as an array. Line 295 documents 'capabilities.toolset -- array of tool identifiers the agent can use'.

results.prompt: I want to test a ServiceNow tool before giving it to my agent. How do I call it directly?

assertions.text: Maps adapter tool identifier to POST /AdapterName/methodName route
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 98-104 provide a table mapping tool type and identifier to direct test route. Line 100 shows adapter 'ServiceNow//createChangeRequest' maps to 'POST /ServiceNow/createChangeRequest'.

assertions.text: Suggests checking openapi.json for the correct request body
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 117-119 show checking openapi.json for ServiceNow endpoints and request body: 'jq .paths["/ServiceNow/createChangeRequest"].post.requestBody openapi.json'.

assertions.text: Tests the tool directly before assigning to agent
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 75-77 state 'Don't give an agent a tool you haven't tested yourself. Every tool is a platform API call -- test it directly first.' Step 5 header is 'Test the tools BEFORE giving them to the agent'.

results.prompt: My agent mission failed. The conclusion says the tool returned an error about missing fields. How do I debug this?

assertions.text: Checks mission details: conclusion, toolStats, tokenUsage
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 190-193 describe checking mission: 'conclusion -- what the agent said at the end', 'toolStats.tools -- which tools were called', 'tokenUsage -- if very high, the agent may be looping'. Lines 714-719 show the debug scenario steps.

assertions.text: Tests the failing tool directly outside the agent
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 195-197 state 'Identify which tool failed' then 'Test that tool directly -- call the same API endpoint with the same parameters the agent used.'

assertions.text: Updates system prompt to guide correct tool usage
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 199-203 show 'Fix the system prompt -- if the agent is passing wrong parameters, add guidance' with concrete examples. Line 205 shows the iterate loop: update agent via PUT, call again, check mission.

results.prompt: Create a quick one-off agent to check if a device is reachable. Don't save it.

assertions.text: Uses adhoc_agent endpoint, not agents create+call
assertions.evidence: Skill line 242 documents 'POST /flowai/adhoc_agent -- Run a one-off agent without saving it'. Lines 334-343 show the full adhoc_agent example.
Skill line 343 states 'Requires default_provider to be set in app properties.' Gotcha on line 588 reiterates: 'adHocAgent requires default_provider set in FlowAI app properties'.

assertions.text: Includes tools array with correct // identifier format
assertions.evidence: Skill line 339 shows 'tools': ['AutomationGateway//sendCommand'] in the adhoc_agent example, using the // identifier format.

results.prompt: I get 'Tool names must be unique' when creating my agent. I have two adapters with getDevice methods.

assertions.text: Identifies duplicate tool names as the cause
assertions.evidence: Skill line 597 documents: '"Tool names must be unique" error -- happens when multiple adapters expose methods with the same name (e.g., getDevice on two adapters). The LLM provider rejects duplicate tool names.' Troubleshooting table line 214 also lists this.

assertions.text: Advises filtering capabilities.toolset to specific adapter
assertions.evidence: Skill line 597 continues: 'Use specific tool identifiers in capabilities.toolset to avoid loading conflicting tools.' Line 214 says 'Remove conflicting tools from capabilities'.

results.prompt: Build me a React app with authentication.

assertions.text: Skill is not triggered for generic web dev tasks
assertions.evidence: The skill description (line 3) scopes it to 'Create and run AI agents on the Itential Platform.' React/web dev is entirely outside this scope. The CLAUDE.md skill router maps flowagent to 'AI Agents' only.
